channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Cum îți citește de fapt motorul de căutare pagina în 2026 (și de ce numărul de cuvinte e irelevant)
item.description: Hai să vorbim despre o neînțelegere care refuză să moară: ideea că optimizezi o pagină numărând de câte ori apare un cuvânt cheie în ea. Nu așa funcționează căutarea de peste un deceniu. Iar cu...

item.title: Why Specialization Is Inevitable
Scientists On AI: It’s Still Experimental
item.description: As the AI Engineer World's Fair Expo kicked off, a group of scientists gathered for an informal get-together to discuss how this new technology is affecting their profession. "I work in chemistry,...

item.title: Gemma, the Epstein Files, and sandboxing cause a stir at the World's Fair
item.description: As the AI Engineer World’s Fair kicked off officially on Monday, the halls were filled for the traditional workshop day, where coders from across the country — and in some cases from around the...

item.title: The Time Spent on Infrastructure Can Easily Surpass the Time Spent Building Features
item.description: One thing I've realized after building several products is this: Sometimes, I spend more time managing infrastructure than actually building features. I've always enjoyed using PocketBase. It's...

item.title: Building an Automated Affiliate Analytics Dashboard with Python
item.description: Affiliate marketing usually comes with dozens of dashboards. One network shows clicks. Another shows conversions. A third shows payouts. After a while you spend more time collecting numbers than...

item.title: Developers Urged To Reach Out And Touch Someone
item.description: Monday’s final presentation was perhaps the most unusual one of the day in that it focused more on feelings than technology. Attendees were told to pick a fellow attendee next to them and talk for...

item.title: The Real Cost of DIY: Building a Voice Agent on Twilio + OpenAI
item.description: In 2024, I decided to build a voice agent from scratch. The plan was simple: use Twilio for telephony, OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text, GPT-4 for the brain, and ElevenLabs for voice synthesis....

item.title: Junior DevOps Engineer Seeks Clarity on Role and Responsibilities for Greater Job Security
item.description: Introduction: The DevOps Dilemma Junior DevOps engineers often find themselves in a paradoxical situation: they’re hired to streamline processes, automate workflows, and ensure system reliability,...

item.title: Designing a Precision Risk and Lot Size Calculator for Prop Firm Traded Accounts
item.description: Many Forex traders blow their funded accounts not because of poor entry signals, but because of simple mathematical errors in lot-size calculations. With daily drawdown limits of 4-5% enforced...

item.title: Client-Server Architecture
item.description: Introduction In today's digital world, almost every application we use from social media and online banking to e-commerce websites relies on the Client–Server Architecture. This architecture...

item.title: GuardFall Exposes Open-Source AI Coding Agents to Decades-Old Shell Injection Risks
item.description: The safety check that is supposed to stop an AI coding agent from running a dangerous command can be walked straight past using a shell trick that has been public for decades. New research from...

item.title: Lumo, Proton’s privacy-focused AI chatbot, gets an upgrade
item.description: Proton's Lumo 2.0 is dropping this week, giving users a broader variety of capabilities.

item.title: iPhone 18 Pro ‘drop test’ leaks get yanked from X
item.description: Videos purporting to show the iPhone 18 Pro undergoing a drop test were removed from X shortly after they surfaced, as spotted earlier by MacRumors. An account imitating leaker EvLeaks was...

item.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
item.description: Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic. In many cases, the path in was visible just...

item.title: The Download: AI “coworkers” and stratospheric internet
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. AI agents are not your “coworkers” Imagine coming in to...

item.title: The Realities of AI Video Surveillance
item.description: The Financial Times has a good article on how AI is changing the capabilities of video surveillance, with information from both Israel/Iran and Russia. I wrote about this sort of thing a few years...

item.title: Agriculture is ready for AI, but its data isn’t
item.description: Artificial intelligence is transforming what is possible in agriculture, but industry leaders should be wary of investing in AI without first laying the groundwork. The use cases are promising,...

item.title: Meet the lawyer who beat Elon Musk — twice
item.description: Watching Elon Musk fulminate at Bill Savitt during Musk v. Altman - the case in which Musk sued Sam Altman and OpenAI instead of seeing a therapist about his AI failures - was a bit like watching...

item.title: What the Numbers Say About FIFA 2026 Cyber Risk
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 opened on June 11. By that date, according to Check Point Research, the fraud infrastructure targeting it had already been built, staged, and partially deployed. Threat...

item.title: Attackers Exploit SimpleHelp CVE-2026-48558 to Deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer
item.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed exploiting a recently disclosed maximum-severity security flaw in SimpleHelp to deliver two previously unreported malware families, TaskWeaver and Djinn...

item.title: This motor could be the future of e-bikes
item.description: Imagine an e-bike motor that lets you select your preferred pedaling cadence and then automatically adjusts the gears to keep your legs spinning at that exact speed, no matter how steep the hill...

item.title: Bernie Sanders Saw This Coming
item.description: For decades, the senator has argued that concentrated wealth threatened American democracy. Now he’s betting that frustration with Big Tech, billionaires, and unchecked AI is reaching a tipping point.

item.title: Building tech in the world’s secret R&D hub
item.description: Apple. Anthropic. Disney Research. Google. Meta. Microsoft. NVIDIA. OpenAI. Few places outside Silicon Valley can claim R&D hubs from all of these companies. Fewer still are concentrated in a city...

item.title: Our best look yet at Samsung’s new wide foldable
item.description: Samsung is expected to unveil its next generation of foldables at a Galaxy Unpacked event next month, but now we know what they might look like, courtesy of some leaked images published by Android...

item.title: AirDrop and Quick Share Flaws Let Nearby Attackers Trigger Crashes and Bypass Checks
item.description: Two researchers have found six security flaws in AirDrop and Quick Share, the wireless features that beam files between nearby devices with no cables or shared network. An attacker within wireless...

item.title: Crypto exchange OKX wants AI agents to hire and pay each other
item.description: OKX is bringing together payments, identity, and reputation into a marketplace for AI agents.

item.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
item.description: Convince an AI browser that it is playing a game, and it can hand over your login details. That is the finding behind BioShocking, a technique from security firm LayerX that tricked six AI...

item.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
item.description: A critical vulnerability in Progress Kemp LoadMaster can let an unauthenticated attacker execute arbitrary commands as root on the appliance by sending a crafted request to its API. The flaw,...

item.title: Unlocking Britain’s next era of productivity: Building a nation of AI trailblazers
item.description: Four illustrated characters representing different professional roles, including a scientist, technician, explorer, and observer.
